words.definition: The killing of one person by another.
words.example: The homicide detectives sealed the scene.

words.definition: The unlawful, premeditated killing of a person.
words.example: He was convicted of murder in the first degree.

words.definition: The unlawful killing of a person without malice aforethought.
words.example: The charge was reduced to manslaughter.

words.definition: The murder of a prominent person, often politically motivated.
words.example: The assassination shocked the nation.
Suicide prevention is a public-health priority.

words.definition: The deliberate killing of a large group, especially of a nation or ethnic group.
words.example: Genocide is prosecuted under international law.
Historical records document tragic cases of infanticide.
Cain committed history's archetypal fratricide.
Sororicide is fortunately rare.
Oedipus is the most famous patricide.
Orestes' matricide drove the Furies to torment him.
Medea's filicide is Greek tragedy's darkest turn.
Uxoricide featured in many nineteenth-century sensational trials.
Mariticide, though rarer than uxoricide, is documented.

words.definition: The killing of one's parent or close relative.
words.example: Roman law reserved unique punishment for parricide.
The regicide of Charles I astonished Europe.
Some philosophers defended tyrannicide as a civic duty.
Deicide is a recurring theme in mythology.

words.definition: The destruction of the natural environment.
words.example: Some jurists propose ecocide as an international crime.

words.definition: A substance that destroys living organisms.
words.example: Farmers rotate biocides to slow resistance.

words.definition: A substance used to destroy plants, especially weeds.
words.example: The herbicide sprayed on the field killed the vines.

words.definition: A substance used to kill insects.
words.example: Insecticide use has declined with integrated pest management.

words.definition: A substance used to destroy pests.
words.example: Pesticide residues are monitored on imported produce.

words.definition: A substance used to kill rodents.
words.example: Rodenticide is used cautiously near pets.
Copper surfaces act as a mild bactericide.
Fungicides protect the harvest from mould.

words.definition: A substance that kills worms, especially parasitic ones.
words.example: Vermicides treat livestock parasites.

words.definition: The destruction of a foetus in the womb.
words.example: Aborticide appears mainly in legal contexts.
Foeticide is treated distinctly in some legal codes.

words.definition: The systematic killing of members of a specific gender.
words.example: Historians document gendercide in several conflicts.

words.definition: The murder of any person or people by a government.
words.example: Political scientists coined democide for state killing.

words.definition: The killing of a group because of its political stance.
words.example: Politicide targeted the intelligentsia during the purges.

words.definition: The killing of women, especially by men.
words.example: Femicide has become a distinct category in criminology.

words.definition: The painless killing of a suffering patient, at their request.
words.example: The parliament debated legalising voluntary euthanasia.

words.definition: The killing of animals for food, or the mass killing of people.
words.example: The slaughter of cattle is strictly regulated.

words.definition: An indiscriminate killing of many people.
words.example: The massacre drew international condemnation.

words.definition: The killing of many people, especially in war.
words.example: The battlefield was a scene of appalling carnage.

words.definition: Savage killing, especially of many people.
words.example: The war's early days brought unimaginable butchery.

words.definition: The carrying out of a death sentence.
words.example: The execution was postponed pending appeal.

words.definition: A killing by mob action without legal sanction.
words.example: Lynching remains a stain on national history.

words.definition: Killing or harming by administering poison.
words.example: Historical poisoning cases fill early forensic textbooks.

words.definition: A physical attack that may or may not cause death.
words.example: The assault was captured on CCTV.

words.definition: To kill in a violent way (often literary).
words.example: Legend claims he slew the dragon.
The hunter dispatched the wounded animal humanely.

words.definition: To eliminate or kill, especially in political contexts.
words.example: The regime liquidated its opponents.

words.definition: A sudden removal of people considered undesirable, often by killing.
words.example: Stalin's purges killed hundreds of thousands.

words.definition: The selective killing of animals to reduce numbers.
words.example: The culling of the deer herd was controversial.

words.definition: The complete destruction of a group.
words.example: The extermination camps epitomised state killing.

words.definition: The killing or wounding of people.
words.example: The rally ended without bloodshed.

words.definition: To kill or offer as a sacrifice, especially by fire.
words.example: He immolated himself in protest.

Termiticide protected the foundation.

Molluscicide baits reduce snails.

Acaricides protect livestock.
Larvicides reduce mosquito breeding.
Ovicides break the pest cycle.
Silvicides thin unwanted species.
Arboricide angered the community.
Floricide is a poetic term.
Populicide is another word for massacre.
Vaticide is rare in modern usage.
Nepoticide appears in old chronicles.
Amicicide shocked the village.
Prolicide is a legal term.
Neonaticide is prosecuted separately.
Senicide has been recorded historically.
Autocide is a modern coinage.
Aborticide is a legal term.
Feoticide is discussed in ethics.
Sororicide featured in the trial.
Filicide is the gravest family crime.

words.definition: One who murders by surprise attack.
words.example: The assassin fled at once.
Ancient law separated the manslayer from murderer.
Police sought the perpetrator.
The slayer confessed.

words.definition: Officer who carries out death penalty.
words.example: The executioner wore black.
The assailant escaped down the alley.
Testimony named the hitman.
The sniper hid on the roof.
History branded him a butcher.
Cutthroats roamed the port.
The poisoner was unmasked.
Detectives hunted the strangler.
The fratricidal war divided the nation.
Patricidal themes fill Greek tragedy.
The regicidal plot failed.

words.definition: Involving mass killing of a group.
words.example: The regime's genocidal policy shocked the world.
Suicidal thoughts require support.
His homicidal rage terrified witnesses.
The badger cull sparked protests.
The purge eliminated dissenters.
